title: calendula arvensis subsp. micrantha
intro: Calendula arvensis subsp. micrantha, also known as small-flowered pot marigold or dwarf pot marigold, is a subspecies of the Calendula arvensis plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and eye irritations. The health benefits of Calendula arvensis subsp. micrantha include soothing and calming irritated skin, reducing inflammation and pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Calendula arvensis subsp. micrantha can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as to promote overall skin health.
